evals.prompt: Choose between WebSocket and SSE for a notification system where the server pushes alerts to browser clients. The app runs behind Nginx and AWS ALB. Justify your choice.
evals.expected_output: SSE recommended with load balancer considerations and implementation

assertions.text: Output recommends SSE over WebSocket for unidirectional server-to-client notifications
Output mentions EventSource API for browser-side SSE
Output discusses Nginx buffering (X-Accel-Buffering: no header)
Output mentions that SSE auto-reconnects unlike WebSocket
Output provides a minimal SSE server implementation with Content-Type: text/event-stream

evals.prompt: Fix this TanStack Query mutation that causes the UI to revert for 300ms: mutationFn updates the server, onSuccess calls invalidateQueries. There is no onMutate handler.
evals.expected_output: Full onMutate/onError/onSettled pattern with cancelQueries and snapshot rollback

assertions.text: Output adds onMutate handler with cancelQueries call
Output saves previous value as snapshot in onMutate
Output applies optimistic update via queryClient.setQueryData in onMutate
Output rolls back to snapshot in onError
Output uses onSettled (not onSuccess) for invalidateQueries to ensure cleanup on both success and error

evals.prompt: Implement exponential backoff reconnection for a WebSocket client. Requirements: start at 1s, max 30s, add jitter, stop after 10 attempts, notify user on failure.
evals.expected_output: Reconnection class with exponential backoff, jitter, max attempts, user notification

assertions.text: Output implements exponential delay: baseDelay * 2^attempt
Output adds random jitter to prevent thundering herd
Output caps delay at maxDelay (30s or similar)
Output tracks attempt counter and stops after max attempts
Output notifies user when max attempts exceeded

evals.prompt: Set up Supabase Realtime CDC subscription for a tasks table in React. Handle INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE events and update the TanStack Query cache for each.
evals.expected_output: useEffect with supabase.channel subscription, event handlers for all 3 operations, cleanup

assertions.text: Output uses supabase.channel().on('postgres_changes', ...).subscribe()
Output handles eventType === 'INSERT' by adding to cache
Output handles eventType === 'UPDATE' by patching cache item
Output handles eventType === 'DELETE' by filtering cache
Output returns cleanup function calling supabase.removeChannel()

evals.prompt: Implement collaborative text editing with Yjs. Two users edit the same document simultaneously. Include: ydoc setup, WebsocketProvider, IndexedDB persistence, and awareness for cursor sharing.
evals.expected_output: Yjs setup with WebsocketProvider, IndexeddbPersistence, Y.Text, and awareness API

assertions.text: Output creates Y.Doc instance
Output sets up WebsocketProvider connected to a sync server
Output uses IndexeddbPersistence for offline support
Output uses Y.getText() for collaborative text
Output uses wsProvider.awareness.setLocalState() for cursor/presence sharing
